2026-04-20.patch_summary: This patch adds a new feature to the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module called passthrough open. When enabled, it allows the kernel to skip sending certain operations like FUSE_OPEN and FUSE_OPENDIR to the server and instead opens the backing file directly. This is done by setting the FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_OP_OPEN flag in the ops mask of the backing map. The patch also includes changes to handle passthrough open for directories, regular files, and atomic truncate operations. It aims to improve performance by reducing the number of operations sent to the server.
